item.title: Run a Restaurant Codes (June 2026)
item.description:
Run a Restaurant is a fun Roblox game where you get to build and run your very own restaurant. You cook tasty recipes, serve hungry customers, decorate your space, raise animals, and grow crops for fresh ingredients. Working codes can give you special decorations for your restaurant that you might have to grind for hours to get naturally!

1

All Run a Restaurant Codes

https://www.roblox.com/games/77843161404023/Run-a-Restaurant https://discord.com/invite/runarestaurant
Roblox Game Info

Roblox Game Info

Loading game data...

— —
Players
—
Visits
—
Favorites
—
View on Roblox

Working Run a Restaurant Codes

Code

Reward(s)

RAR4EVER

Painting

Expired Run a Restaurant Codes

There are no expired codes right now. Every code that has been released for this Roblox game is still working, but it's best to redeem them before they are no longer available!

A code that provides a free painting lets you make your place look nicer without waiting or buying things. Nicer decorations can help draw in more customers and make the whole experience more fun. More customers mean more sales, which means newer recipes and a superior restaurant!

1

How to Redeem Run a Restaurant Codes

  • First, open Run a Restaurant on Roblox and join your server.
  • Click the small cog icon in the top left corner to open the settings menu.
  • Scroll down until you see the code box near the bottom.
  • Type or paste the code exactly as it appears.
  • Click the Submit button.

If the code works, you will get a message, and your reward will appear in your inventory or restaurant right away.

How to Get More Run a Restaurant Codes

New promo codes usually come out when the developers add updates or hit special goals. The best place to hear about them first is the developers' official Discord server. You can also visit the Burnt Toast Labs group page on Roblox for any news they share there.

We update this article regularly with fresh working codes as soon as they appear, so checking back here every so often is the easiest way to stay ahead and grab every free reward before they are gone.

Roblox
Released
September 1, 2006

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

ESRB
Teen / Diverse Content: Discretion Advised, In-Game Purchases (Includes Random Items), Users Interact
Genre(s)
Game Creation System
Platform(s)
PlayStation 4, Xbox One, PC, Android, iOS, Meta Quest 2, Meta Quest Pro
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
Run a Restaurant is a fun Roblox game where you get to build and run your very own restaurant. You cook tasty recipes, serve hungry customers, decorate your space, raise animals, and grow crops for fresh ingredients. Working codes can give you special decorations for your restaurant that you might have to grind for hours to get naturally!

There are no expired codes right now. Every code that has been released for this Roblox game is still working, but it's best to redeem them before they are no longer available!

A code that provides a free painting lets you make your place look nicer without waiting or buying things. Nicer decorations can help draw in more customers and make the whole experience more fun. More customers mean more sales, which means newer recipes and a superior restaurant!

1

How to Redeem Run a Restaurant Codes

  • First, open Run a Restaurant on Roblox and join your server.
  • Click the small cog icon in the top left corner to open the settings menu.
  • Scroll down until you see the code box near the bottom.
  • Type or paste the code exactly as it appears.
  • Click the Submit button.

If the code works, you will get a message, and your reward will appear in your inventory or restaurant right away.

How to Get More Run a Restaurant Codes

New promo codes usually come out when the developers add updates or hit special goals. The best place to hear about them first is the developers' official Discord server. You can also visit the Burnt Toast Labs group page on Roblox for any news they share there.

We update this article regularly with fresh working codes as soon as they appear, so checking back here every so often is the easiest way to stay ahead and grab every free reward before they are gone.

item.title: Someone Just Turned $25 Into $3 Million Using NES Super Mario Bros.
item.description:
Someone has effectively exchanged a game originally worth just $25 for $3 million by way of a copy of the original Super Mario Bros. for the Nintendo Entertainment System. This copy of Super Mario Bros. has broken a number of records, and likely left the original owner extremely pleased at the result of its time at auction.

While not every game is destined to become collectible, those who are patient and have games carefully tucked away somewhere safe can sometimes turn those into a significant profit. In the past, auctioned games have sold for tens or even hundreds of thousands of dollars, depending upon their condition and rarity. However, this new accomplishment leaves all of them in the dust.

Super Mario Bros Sells for a Whopping $3 Million at Auction

On June 12, a copy of Super Mario Bros. for the NES successfully sold for $3 million USD at Heritage Auctions. The stunning feat was due to the game being in excellent condition, and for being an extremely rare copy. When sold separately back in the day, Super Mario Bros. sold for $25, which would be worth about $77 today due to inflation. This particular copy of Super Mario Bros. featured the gloss sticker seal, rather than a matte sticker or shrink-wrap sealing the game box, making it a second production copy.

What's That Weapon?

Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.

5.0 s
0 / 10

Results

0

High Score: 0

—

More Games
What's That Weapon? Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.
Easy (7.5s)Medium (5.0s)Hard (2.5s)Permadeath (2.5s)
5.0 s
0 / 10
Results
0
High Score: 0 —
More Games

Only three sealed copies of the game with this particular sticker seal are known to exist, and the $3 million copy that just sold is in the best condition of them all, with a PSA 9.6 A++ grade. It was previously estimated that a maximum of 10,000 copies with this particular seal ever existed, and potentially as few as 2,000. As a result, even finding one in poor condition would be a difficult task 40 years on from Super Mario Bros. ' original release.

While the sale is remarkable, this isn't the first time that Super Mario Bros. has broken records like this. One of the aforementioned gloss sticker seal copies of Super Mario Bros. broke records in 2019 when it sold for six digits, while a differently-packaged version of the same game ended up selling for $660,000 just two years later. That record was ultimately beaten that same year by The Legend of Zelda , which sold for $870,000, and many more records have been established since. However, this new $3 million sale means that Super Mario Bros. has taken its crown back, and become the most expensive game ever sold in the history of video gaming.

Find all 10 pairs

Results

0
—
More Games
Find all 10 pairs
Regular Cover ArtClean Cover Art
Results
0
—
More Games

Learning that someone spent seven digits on a video game may be stunning, but it's far from the only instance of game fans spending tons on their favorites. Outside of copies of video games, things like Pokemon TCG cards have racked up millions of dollars in sales, with a rare Pikachu Illustrator selling for $1.4 million earlier in 2026, and a Logan Paul-owned copy of the same card selling for $16.5 million. Meanwhile, a seller put one of the Space World 2000 GameCube prototypes on sale for $100,000, which, while historical, doesn't actually contain any internal components, so it doesn't function as a real console.

Super Mario Bros.

Follow
Followed
Platformer
Systems
Released
November 17, 1985
ESRB
E For Everyone
Developer(s)
Nintendo R&D4
Publisher(s)
Nintendo
Franchise
Super Mario
6 Images

WHERE TO PLAY

PHYSICAL

Genre(s)
Platformer
Powered by

Someone has effectively exchanged a game originally worth just $25 for $3 million by way of a copy of the original Super Mario Bros. for the Nintendo Entertainment System. This copy of Super Mario Bros. has broken a number of records, and likely left the original owner extremely pleased at the result of its time at auction.

While not every game is destined to become collectible, those who are patient and have games carefully tucked away somewhere safe can sometimes turn those into a significant profit. In the past, auctioned games have sold for tens or even hundreds of thousands of dollars, depending upon their condition and rarity. However, this new accomplishment leaves all of them in the dust.

Super Mario Bros Sells for a Whopping $3 Million at Auction

On June 12, a copy of Super Mario Bros. for the NES successfully sold for $3 million USD at Heritage Auctions. The stunning feat was due to the game being in excellent condition, and for being an extremely rare copy. When sold separately back in the day, Super Mario Bros. sold for $25, which would be worth about $77 today due to inflation. This particular copy of Super Mario Bros. featured the gloss sticker seal, rather than a matte sticker or shrink-wrap sealing the game box, making it a second production copy.

Only three sealed copies of the game with this particular sticker seal are known to exist, and the $3 million copy that just sold is in the best condition of them all, with a PSA 9.6 A++ grade. It was previously estimated that a maximum of 10,000 copies with this particular seal ever existed, and potentially as few as 2,000. As a result, even finding one in poor condition would be a difficult task 40 years on from Super Mario Bros. ' original release.

While the sale is remarkable, this isn't the first time that Super Mario Bros. has broken records like this. One of the aforementioned gloss sticker seal copies of Super Mario Bros. broke records in 2019 when it sold for six digits, while a differently-packaged version of the same game ended up selling for $660,000 just two years later. That record was ultimately beaten that same year by The Legend of Zelda , which sold for $870,000, and many more records have been established since. Learning that someone spent seven digits on a video game may be stunning, but it's far from the only instance of game fans spending tons on their favorites. Outside of copies of video games, things like Pokemon TCG cards have racked up millions of dollars in sales, with a rare Pikachu Illustrator selling for $1.4 million earlier in 2026, and a Logan Paul-owned copy of the same card selling for $16.5 million. item.title: 5 Open-World Games Of This Generation That Are Legit Flawless
item.description:
The open-world genre has often served as something of a benchmark for how far gaming has come as a medium. Combining massive and immersive seamless virtual worlds, cinematic narratives driven by memorable characters, and exciting exploration full of freedom and adventure in one alluring package, some of the most iconic and acclaimed games of all time belong to the open-world family. From Grand Theft Auto and Red Dead Redemption 2 to The Witcher 3 and Elden Ring — it’s impossible to deny these games’ global impact.

The eighth generation of video game consoles (PS4 & Xbox One) was among the most abundant periods for open-world games, with many of the most popular titles released and modern franchises established. But what about the current generation so far? While the upcoming GTA 6 is already poised to shake up the entire industry in just a few months, let’s explore some of the best open-world games released during the ninth console generation. While the “flawless” part is definitely subjective, the games below are some of the highest-rated over the past few years, representing the top-tier offerings from the modern open-world lexicon.

Just to make it absolutely clear, the focus here is only on games released natively on PS5 and Xbox Series X/S, and not available on last-gen devices .

Ghost Of Yotei

Perfecting An Already Unique Open-World DNA

Sucker Punch’s unique approach to its samurai-focused action-adventure series resulted in subsequent games that don’t have numbers in their titles. These entries feel almost standalone in their appeal, bringing original stories and characters with each new game and not requiring players to experience its predecessors. While Ghost of Tsushima’s DNA is unmistakable when playing Ghost of Yotei , the new game managed to improve on almost everything from the original, perfecting the series’ signature flow of brutal action, tight narratives, and the true spirit of adventure in a drop-dead gorgeous virtual Japan.

Ghost of Yotei works perfectly no matter how players approach it. Whether you look for a condensed, cinematic story centered on action and narrative with little to no fluff or, on the contrary, value its less curated free-roam side — it’s the near-perfect blend of the two. Add some of the prettiest visuals in the genre this generation and combat as deep and satisfying as it is flashy and approachable, and you’ve got an alluring mix of a game that’s hard to put down. In the realm of modern open-world titles, Ghost of Yotei is truly flawless, fulfilling its promise to be the ultimate sword-wielding adventure.

Forza Horizon 6

You Don’t Even Need To Love Cars To Love This

It’s amazing how many modern games (and not just open-world titles) are set in Japan these days, but what’s even more amusing is how we still haven’t had enough of it. Unlike the historical side of the country, though, Forza Horizon 6 embraces its contemporary allure, delivering the setting that series fans have been craving for years, and doing it with a bang. Even if FH6 is very light on innovation and offers mostly minor evolutionary changes, its setting and map alone are enough to hook even players who are usually indifferent to driving sims.

And it’s easy to see why: every minute spent in Forza Horizon 6 is a great time, so why change things too much? Tons of side activities and racing events, hundreds of detailed and customizable cars, flexible difficulty, and ongoing seasonal challenges and game modes. All that aside, just cruising around a beautifully realized Japan, listening to an awesome score no matter the radio station, gathering fun collectibles, and enjoying smooth driving feels almost like therapy after a long day at work. Forza Horizon 6 is an absolute masterclass in its own subgenre, simply unreachable by the competition for almost a decade now.

Build your perfect top ten one reveal at a time.

—

—

Your Ranking

—

0 / 10
Pick a spot for this card.

Final Ranking

—

Your blind ranking is locked in.

More Games

Swap two picks?

Pick two spots to swap before locking in your final ranking.

Build your perfect top ten one reveal at a time.

—

—
Your Ranking —
0 / 10
Pick a spot for this card.
Final Ranking
—
Your blind ranking is locked in.
More Games
Swap two picks? Pick two spots to swap before locking in your final ranking.

Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2

This Gen’s Ultimate Open-World Medieval RPG

Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 represents one of the biggest leaps in quality across the board from an original game to its sequel, standing head and shoulders above the first entry. In fact, the game has already garnered a reputation as this generation’s ultimate open-world medieval RPG, standing side by side with titans like The Witcher 3 or Skyrim . On top of that, Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 represents one of the most immersive games ever made, competing with Red Dead Redemption 2 in this regard, and delivering a world and characters that feel alive even when you do nothing while exploring Bohemia.

The biggest achievement with Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 , though, is how thoroughly the developers addressed all the issues from the original, fully capitalizing on its unique promise. The sequel isn’t just a better game in every regard; it’s miles more fun in its story and gameplay flow, achieving just the right balance of meticulous attention to historical detail and an engaging moment-to-moment presence in this world as Henry. Everything you do or say really matters in KCD 2 , while the central narrative is so tight this time around that it simply grabs you by the neck and pulls you in for an unforgettable journey.

Death Stranding 2: On The Beach

Not A Revolution, But A Perfect Refinement

Death Stranding 2: On the Beach is yet another example of the total domination of open-world sequels in this era of gaming when compared to last-gen, where many of those popular franchises were founded in the first place. When it comes to Death Stranding 2 , many fans were surprised by the sequel’s announcement, thinking that the game was unnecessary and wouldn’t be able to add anything truly substantial to Hideo Kojima’s own established “strand” subgenre. Upon its arrival, though, Death Stranding 2: On the Beach proved there’s still tons of potential left in this fascinating series, bringing both the gameplay and visuals to new heights while notably enriching its core formula in every way.

When compared to the original, Death Stranding 2: On the Beach is a much more freedom-focused and flexible game, entrusting players with tons of traversal and building tools and even vehicles right from the get-go, and allowing them to truly carve their own path through its vast new territories of post-apocalyptic Australia, full of distinct biomes. With the basics already established, like any decent sequel, DS2 focuses instead on making its moment-to-moment interactions more fun, tossing in some truly spectacular scenes, creative episodes, and unforgettable boss encounters.

Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth

Its Only Flaw Is Being Way Too Generous

Just like Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 , Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth embodies an astounding step up in everything when compared to the already acclaimed FF7 Remake . From the sheer size of the world and a generational leap in visuals to a much bigger array of activities, a more nuanced story and characters, and drastically improved exploration and combat — Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth is almost twice as long, and, as some say, almost twice as fun. In many respects, it’s the kind of game that studios just don’t make anymore, combining a winning old-school approach to story and nostalgia with all the modern achievements to deliver the best of both worlds.

With FF7 Rebirth , the only possible issue for some is that Square Enix literally over-delivers in trying to make the game as big as it is, packing so many side quests, plotlines, and activities into a single title. Of course, it’s hardly a shortcoming for most players, especially given how modern gamers constantly calculate value based on a game’s length. Trust me: you won’t be sold short with this one. With Final Fantasy 7 Revelation already announced and shaping up to be even more ambitious and grander than this spectacular middle chapter, now is probably the best time to dive into this classic reimagining that’ll undoubtedly take its place as one of the best trilogies ever made.
item.content:encoded:
The open-world genre has often served as something of a benchmark for how far gaming has come as a medium. Combining massive and immersive seamless virtual worlds, cinematic narratives driven by memorable characters, and exciting exploration full of freedom and adventure in one alluring package, some of the most iconic and acclaimed games of all time belong to the open-world family. From Grand Theft Auto and Red Dead Redemption 2 to The Witcher 3 and Elden Ring — it’s impossible to deny these games’ global impact.

The eighth generation of video game consoles (PS4 & Xbox One) was among the most abundant periods for open-world games, with many of the most popular titles released and modern franchises established. But what about the current generation so far? While the upcoming GTA 6 is already poised to shake up the entire industry in just a few months, let’s explore some of the best open-world games released during the ninth console generation. While the “flawless” part is definitely subjective, the games below are some of the highest-rated over the past few years, representing the top-tier offerings from the modern open-world lexicon.

Just to make it absolutely clear, the focus here is only on games released natively on PS5 and Xbox Series X/S, and not available on last-gen devices .

Ghost Of Yotei

Perfecting An Already Unique Open-World DNA

Sucker Punch’s unique approach to its samurai-focused action-adventure series resulted in subsequent games that don’t have numbers in their titles. These entries feel almost standalone in their appeal, bringing original stories and characters with each new game and not requiring players to experience its predecessors. While Ghost of Tsushima’s DNA is unmistakable when playing Ghost of Yotei , the new game managed to improve on almost everything from the original, perfecting the series’ signature flow of brutal action, tight narratives, and the true spirit of adventure in a drop-dead gorgeous virtual Japan.

Ghost of Yotei works perfectly no matter how players approach it. Whether you look for a condensed, cinematic story centered on action and narrative with little to no fluff or, on the contrary, value its less curated free-roam side — it’s the near-perfect blend of the two. Add some of the prettiest visuals in the genre this generation and combat as deep and satisfying as it is flashy and approachable, and you’ve got an alluring mix of a game that’s hard to put down. In the realm of modern open-world titles, Ghost of Yotei is truly flawless, fulfilling its promise to be the ultimate sword-wielding adventure.

You Don’t Even Need To Love Cars To Love This

It’s amazing how many modern games (and not just open-world titles) are set in Japan these days, but what’s even more amusing is how we still haven’t had enough of it. Unlike the historical side of the country, though, Forza Horizon 6 embraces its contemporary allure, delivering the setting that series fans have been craving for years, and doing it with a bang. Even if FH6 is very light on innovation and offers mostly minor evolutionary changes, its setting and map alone are enough to hook even players who are usually indifferent to driving sims.

And it’s easy to see why: every minute spent in Forza Horizon 6 is a great time, so why change things too much? Tons of side activities and racing events, hundreds of detailed and customizable cars, flexible difficulty, and ongoing seasonal challenges and game modes. All that aside, just cruising around a beautifully realized Japan, listening to an awesome score no matter the radio station, gathering fun collectibles, and enjoying smooth driving feels almost like therapy after a long day at work. Forza Horizon 6 is an absolute masterclass in its own subgenre, simply unreachable by the competition for almost a decade now.

Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2

This Gen’s Ultimate Open-World Medieval RPG

Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 represents one of the biggest leaps in quality across the board from an original game to its sequel, standing head and shoulders above the first entry. In fact, the game has already garnered a reputation as this generation’s ultimate open-world medieval RPG, standing side by side with titans like The Witcher 3 or Skyrim . On top of that, Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 represents one of the most immersive games ever made, competing with Red Dead Redemption 2 in this regard, and delivering a world and characters that feel alive even when you do nothing while exploring Bohemia.

The biggest achievement with Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 , though, is how thoroughly the developers addressed all the issues from the original, fully capitalizing on its unique promise. The sequel isn’t just a better game in every regard; it’s miles more fun in its story and gameplay flow, achieving just the right balance of meticulous attention to historical detail and an engaging moment-to-moment presence in this world as Henry. Everything you do or say really matters in KCD 2 , while the central narrative is so tight this time around that it simply grabs you by the neck and pulls you in for an unforgettable journey.

Not A Revolution, But A Perfect Refinement

Death Stranding 2: On the Beach is yet another example of the total domination of open-world sequels in this era of gaming when compared to last-gen, where many of those popular franchises were founded in the first place. When it comes to Death Stranding 2 , many fans were surprised by the sequel’s announcement, thinking that the game was unnecessary and wouldn’t be able to add anything truly substantial to Hideo Kojima’s own established “strand” subgenre. Upon its arrival, though, Death Stranding 2: On the Beach proved there’s still tons of potential left in this fascinating series, bringing both the gameplay and visuals to new heights while notably enriching its core formula in every way.

When compared to the original, Death Stranding 2: On the Beach is a much more freedom-focused and flexible game, entrusting players with tons of traversal and building tools and even vehicles right from the get-go, and allowing them to truly carve their own path through its vast new territories of post-apocalyptic Australia, full of distinct biomes. With the basics already established, like any decent sequel, DS2 focuses instead on making its moment-to-moment interactions more fun, tossing in some truly spectacular scenes, creative episodes, and unforgettable boss encounters.

Its Only Flaw Is Being Way Too Generous

Just like Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 , Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth embodies an astounding step up in everything when compared to the already acclaimed FF7 Remake . From the sheer size of the world and a generational leap in visuals to a much bigger array of activities, a more nuanced story and characters, and drastically improved exploration and combat — Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth is almost twice as long, and, as some say, almost twice as fun. In many respects, it’s the kind of game that studios just don’t make anymore, combining a winning old-school approach to story and nostalgia with all the modern achievements to deliver the best of both worlds.

With FF7 Rebirth , the only possible issue for some is that Square Enix literally over-delivers in trying to make the game as big as it is, packing so many side quests, plotlines, and activities into a single title. Of course, it’s hardly a shortcoming for most players, especially given how modern gamers constantly calculate value based on a game’s length. Trust me: you won’t be sold short with this one. With Final Fantasy 7 Revelation already announced and shaping up to be even more ambitious and grander than this spectacular middle chapter, now is probably the best time to dive into this classic reimagining that’ll undoubtedly take its place as one of the best trilogies ever made.

item.title: Persona 4 Revival: Where You Know its Voice Cast From
item.description:
We all knew it was coming, but at the recent Xbox Games Showcase, Atlus officially offered a look at Persona 4 Revival , the remake of 2008's Persona 4 . In essence, it appears to follow the same path as Persona 3 Reload , serving as a full-fledged reimagining of the original title rather than a simple remaster or revamp a la Persona 5 Royal .

Persona 4 is often cited as the best in the franchise, and it's not hard to see why. While Persona 3 and Persona 5 both take place in cities, Persona 4 sets itself apart by taking place in a rural town called Inaba, setting it apart in an immediate and significant way. The TV World is also a delightful and creative spin on the "otherworld" convention present in every other Persona title. But at the end of the day, Persona 4 is still a Persona story, meaning that it’s driven by a cast of lovable party members. Thankfully, it looks like this party has been paired with a strong voice cast for Persona 4 Revival .

The following only covers the English cast for Persona 4 Revival , not the original Japanese.

Protagonist: Nazeeh Tarsha (Street Fighter 6, Genshin Impact, Zenless Zone Zero)

As is the case with most Atlus RPGs, Persona 4 's protagonist is a man of few words, his dialog mostly boiling down to combat barks and one-liners. But these are still important to get right—players will be hearing them countless times over the course of dozens of hours, after all.

Build your perfect top ten one reveal at a time.

—

—

Your Ranking

—

0 / 10
Pick a spot for this card.

Final Ranking

—

Your blind ranking is locked in.

More Games

Swap two picks?

Pick two spots to swap before locking in your final ranking.

Build your perfect top ten one reveal at a time.

—

—
Your Ranking —
0 / 10
Pick a spot for this card.
Final Ranking
—
Your blind ranking is locked in.
More Games
Swap two picks? Pick two spots to swap before locking in your final ranking.

As you might expect, Atlus nabbed a solid VA for this leading role. Nazeeh Tarsha has been involved in over 200 productions, most of them either gaming-or animation-related, with his most notable roles including the flamboyant twins Uou and Sao from Street Fighter 6 , bookish and brilliant Alhaitham from Genshin Impact , and defense-oriented cat man Seth in Zenless Zone Zero . He has also appeared in a number of prominent anime. For instance, he was the English voice of Kafka Hibino in Kaiju No. 8 , and he also recently voiced Gaul in the excellent Monster Hunter Stories 3 .

Yosuke Hanamura: Paul Castro Jr. (NEO: The World Ends with You, Daemon X Machina: Titanic Scion, Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth)

Filling the traditonal best friend role is the cheery but deceptively complex Yosuke Hanamura, played by the esteemed Paul Castro Jr. Castro Jr. has played rambunctious and energetic young men with boyish charm in the past, so he seems like a good fit for this role.

His best-known gaming role is probably Rindo Kanade, protagonist of the 2022 action-RPG NEO: The World Ends with You . More recently, he turned in a strong performance as Chocobo Billy in FF7 Rebirth , one of many roles that were fleshed out for the FF7 remake saga. He also played a major role in Daemon X Machina: Titanic Scion , voicing the rugged and gruff Ash. Other prominent roles include Heartman's AED in Death Stranding 2 , Eoren in Visions of Mana , and Freminet in Genshin Impact .

Chie Satonaka: Anne Yatco (Genshin Impact, Jujutsu Kaisen, Date Everything!)

A personal favorite of mine, Chie Satonaka is Persona 4 's most outwardly aggressive and protective cast member. She's full of energy and has a strong sense of justice which, coupled with her obsession with martial arts, makes her a one-of-a-kind party member, and easily one of the best parts of the original Persona 4 .

For the role of Satonaka, Atlus has tapped experienced VA Anne Yatco, best known for her performance as Nobara Kugisaki in Jujutsu Kaisen . Her most well-known video game role is arguably Raiden Shogun, one of the strongest playable characters in Genshin Impact . However, her resume is far more extensive than that, as she's been featured in hits like Naraka Bladepoint (Kurumi) and Ghostwire Tokyo (Mari) as well. She’s also another NEO: The World Ends with You alumnus, having played Sakura, Yotsuba, and Aika in that game.

Yukiko Amagi: Brianna Knickerbocker (Demon Slayer, Shenmue 3, Fire Emblem: Fates)

Yukiko Amagi is Chie Satonaka's best friend and counterpart, since Yukiko herself is reserved, prim, and traditionally ladylike—a stark contrast to Chie's brash irresponsibility. But she's also much deeper and more conflicted than her cool, shy persona might suggest, and her complicated relationship with Chie is one of Persona 4 ’s highlights.

Here, Atlus has gone with Brianna Knickerbocker, another accomplished voice actress best known for her performance as major character Kanao Tsuyuri in Demon Slayer . She has numerous video game credits as well, with notable examples including the player-character in Code Vein 2 , Saoirse in Octopath Traveler 2 , and Sakura in Fire Emblem: Fates . She's also rubbed shoulders with some of her Persona 4 co-stars, having played Holly in Date Everything! and Hu Tao in Genshin Impact .

Marie: Ari Thrash (Honkai Star Rail, Mobile Suit Gundam, Dust Queen)

Persona 4 Revival isn't actually the first time that Persona 4 has been re-released, having previously been updated and revamped with Persona 4 Golden on the PS Vita. Much like Persona 5 Royal , Persona 4 Golden adds Marie, a new major character, to the mix. Marie isn't playable herself, but her role as a Velvet Room assistant enables her to help the protagonist grow more powerful. She's also crucial for Persona 4 Golden 's online features.

It's somewhat fitting that this role has gone to Ari Thrash, the most junior VA in the Persona 4 Revival cast. Only in the field for a few years now, Thrash has already grown an impressive resume, voicing the recently-added Yao Guang in Honkai Star Rail and Deux Murasame in the anime Mobile Suit Gundam GQuuuuuuX . Hopefully, both she and Marie will get ample opportunities to shine in Revival .

Persona 4 Revival

Follow
Followed
JRPG
Simulation
Adventure
Systems
Released
February 18, 2027
Developer(s)
Atlus
Publisher(s)
Sega
Franchise
Persona
PC Release Date
February 18, 2027
4 Images

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Persona 4 Revival intertwines an investigation into a string of murders with the journey of self-discovery and encounters with the occult, all within a critically acclaimed, classic RPG experience—reborn in a new form!

In the rural Japanese town of Inaba, the once sleepy streets receive a cold wake-up call when a mysterious serial killer strikes. Also, what's with the rumor of a mysterious TV channel appearing only on rainy nights? A whirlwind of surprises and otherworldly adventures will make the next year of your high school life anything but ordinary!

Xbox Series X|S Release Date
February 18, 2027
PS5 Release Date
February 18, 2027
Genre(s)
JRPG, Simulation, Adventure
Platform(s)
PC, PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X, Xbox Series S
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
We all knew it was coming, but at the recent Xbox Games Showcase, Atlus officially offered a look at Persona 4 Revival , the remake of 2008's Persona 4 . In essence, it appears to follow the same path as Persona 3 Reload , serving as a full-fledged reimagining of the original title rather than a simple remaster or revamp a la Persona 5 Royal .

Persona 4 is often cited as the best in the franchise, and it's not hard to see why. While Persona 3 and Persona 5 both take place in cities, Persona 4 sets itself apart by taking place in a rural town called Inaba, setting it apart in an immediate and significant way. The TV World is also a delightful and creative spin on the "otherworld" convention present in every other Persona title. But at the end of the day, Persona 4 is still a Persona story, meaning that it’s driven by a cast of lovable party members. Thankfully, it looks like this party has been paired with a strong voice cast for Persona 4 Revival .

The following only covers the English cast for Persona 4 Revival , not the original Japanese.

Protagonist: Nazeeh Tarsha (Street Fighter 6, Genshin Impact, Zenless Zone Zero)

As is the case with most Atlus RPGs, Persona 4 's protagonist is a man of few words, his dialog mostly boiling down to combat barks and one-liners. But these are still important to get right—players will be hearing them countless times over the course of dozens of hours, after all.

Build your perfect top ten one reveal at a time.

—

—

Your Ranking

—

0 / 10
Pick a spot for this card.

Final Ranking

—

Your blind ranking is locked in.

More Games

Swap two picks?

Pick two spots to swap before locking in your final ranking.

Build your perfect top ten one reveal at a time.

—

—
Your Ranking —
0 / 10
Pick a spot for this card.
Final Ranking
—
Your blind ranking is locked in.
More Games
Swap two picks? Pick two spots to swap before locking in your final ranking.

As you might expect, Atlus nabbed a solid VA for this leading role. Nazeeh Tarsha has been involved in over 200 productions, most of them either gaming-or animation-related, with his most notable roles including the flamboyant twins Uou and Sao from Street Fighter 6 , bookish and brilliant Alhaitham from Genshin Impact , and defense-oriented cat man Seth in Zenless Zone Zero . He has also appeared in a number of prominent anime. For instance, he was the English voice of Kafka Hibino in Kaiju No. 8 , and he also recently voiced Gaul in the excellent Monster Hunter Stories 3 .

Yosuke Hanamura: Paul Castro Jr. (NEO: The World Ends with You, Daemon X Machina: Titanic Scion, Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth)

Filling the traditonal best friend role is the cheery but deceptively complex Yosuke Hanamura, played by the esteemed Paul Castro Jr. Castro Jr. has played rambunctious and energetic young men with boyish charm in the past, so he seems like a good fit for this role.

His best-known gaming role is probably Rindo Kanade, protagonist of the 2022 action-RPG NEO: The World Ends with You . More recently, he turned in a strong performance as Chocobo Billy in FF7 Rebirth , one of many roles that were fleshed out for the FF7 remake saga. He also played a major role in Daemon X Machina: Titanic Scion , voicing the rugged and gruff Ash. Other prominent roles include Heartman's AED in Death Stranding 2 , Eoren in Visions of Mana , and Freminet in Genshin Impact .

Chie Satonaka: Anne Yatco (Genshin Impact, Jujutsu Kaisen, Date Everything!)

A personal favorite of mine, Chie Satonaka is Persona 4 's most outwardly aggressive and protective cast member. She's full of energy and has a strong sense of justice which, coupled with her obsession with martial arts, makes her a one-of-a-kind party member, and easily one of the best parts of the original Persona 4 .

For the role of Satonaka, Atlus has tapped experienced VA Anne Yatco, best known for her performance as Nobara Kugisaki in Jujutsu Kaisen . Her most well-known video game role is arguably Raiden Shogun, one of the strongest playable characters in Genshin Impact . However, her resume is far more extensive than that, as she's been featured in hits like Naraka Bladepoint (Kurumi) and Ghostwire Tokyo (Mari) as well. She’s also another NEO: The World Ends with You alumnus, having played Sakura, Yotsuba, and Aika in that game.

Yukiko Amagi: Brianna Knickerbocker (Demon Slayer, Shenmue 3, Fire Emblem: Fates)

Yukiko Amagi is Chie Satonaka's best friend and counterpart, since Yukiko herself is reserved, prim, and traditionally ladylike—a stark contrast to Chie's brash irresponsibility. But she's also much deeper and more conflicted than her cool, shy persona might suggest, and her complicated relationship with Chie is one of Persona 4 ’s highlights.

Here, Atlus has gone with Brianna Knickerbocker, another accomplished voice actress best known for her performance as major character Kanao Tsuyuri in Demon Slayer . She has numerous video game credits as well, with notable examples including the player-character in Code Vein 2 , Saoirse in Octopath Traveler 2 , and Sakura in Fire Emblem: Fates . She's also rubbed shoulders with some of her Persona 4 co-stars, having played Holly in Date Everything! and Hu Tao in Genshin Impact .

Marie: Ari Thrash (Honkai Star Rail, Mobile Suit Gundam, Dust Queen)

Persona 4 Revival isn't actually the first time that Persona 4 has been re-released, having previously been updated and revamped with Persona 4 Golden on the PS Vita. Much like Persona 5 Royal , Persona 4 Golden adds Marie, a new major character, to the mix. Marie isn't playable herself, but her role as a Velvet Room assistant enables her to help the protagonist grow more powerful. She's also crucial for Persona 4 Golden 's online features.

It's somewhat fitting that this role has gone to Ari Thrash, the most junior VA in the Persona 4 Revival cast. Only in the field for a few years now, Thrash has already grown an impressive resume, voicing the recently-added Yao Guang in Honkai Star Rail and Deux Murasame in the anime Mobile Suit Gundam GQuuuuuuX . Hopefully, both she and Marie will get ample opportunities to shine in Revival .

Persona 4 Revival

Follow
Followed
JRPG
Simulation
Adventure
Systems
Released
February 18, 2027
Developer(s)
Atlus
Publisher(s)
Sega
Franchise
Persona
PC Release Date
February 18, 2027
4 Images

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Persona 4 Revival intertwines an investigation into a string of murders with the journey of self-discovery and encounters with the occult, all within a critically acclaimed, classic RPG experience—reborn in a new form!

In the rural Japanese town of Inaba, the once sleepy streets receive a cold wake-up call when a mysterious serial killer strikes. Also, what's with the rumor of a mysterious TV channel appearing only on rainy nights? A whirlwind of surprises and otherworldly adventures will make the next year of your high school life anything but ordinary!

Xbox Series X|S Release Date
February 18, 2027
PS5 Release Date
February 18, 2027
Genre(s)
JRPG, Simulation, Adventure
Platform(s)
PC, PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X, Xbox Series S
Powered by

item.title: Dead By Daylight: Best Builds for The Spirit (2026)
item.description:
Updated on June 18, 2026 by Mark Sammut: While Rin's kit remains the same, patch 9.6.0 introduced widespread changes that impact Spirit builds. We have added a section discussing these changes, along with tweaking the Beginner's Build and Add-Ons.

Dead by Daylight has over 40 Killers at the moment, and The Spirit has been around longer than the vast majority of them. While seemingly inspired by Japanese horror movies, Rin Yamaoka is an original character, making her more accessible than the licensed Killers. Debuting in 2018's Chapter 9, The Spirit is a staple of DBD 's gameplay scene, and she tends to show up in a decent number of matches. Considering she has not received a dedicated update since 2023, this shows that Rin is just an all-around consistent Killer who has carved out a niche for herself, one that does not need to be tweaked to make her a viable option.

The Spirit can vanish, throwing the Survivors off their tracking game, forcing them to rely on a different sense than usual. If you are a fairly new DBD player and are interested in giving Rin a try, you might need a crash course in her non-teachable perks to get you going with a beginner-friendly build. Once you spend time with the character and unlock further options, you will need to switch to more advanced The Spirit builds.

The Spirit: 2026 Changes

Dead by Daylight's Meta Went Through a Shake-Up Earlier In 2026

Behaviour Interactive hasn't directly updated Rin Yamaoka's baseline mechanics in years, and that seems unlikely to change anytime soon. However, in 2026, the developer implemented changes that impacted nearly every Killer and Survivor, albeit to different extents. The 9.6.0 patch was the biggest DBD update in a long time. Let's go over the main changes.

  • Stacking repeat modifiers now results in diminishing returns. Consequently, stacking multiple identical progression or healing slowdown mechanics now triggers harsh, progressive diminishing returns. The old-school passive Spirit loadouts that stacked slowdowns upon each other have lost a lot of their value. Nowadays, Spirit players should aim for an aggressive, high-tempo chase.
  • From now on, the Killer's identity is hidden at the start of a match, only being revealed to the Survivors once they enter a chase or lose a health state. For a high-mobility threat like Spirit, this is a massive early-game buffer; you can phase directly onto a generator at second zero and execute a clean ambush before survivors even realize they are playing against a phase-walking killer.

The Spirit: Best Beginner's Build

Straightforward Build For New Players

Though a strong Killer, The Spirit comes with a few impressive base kit perks, forcing her to rely on more tried and tested means of killing Survivors. Those just starting might not want to spend money or grind dozens of hours to build a great loadout for a Killer they might not even commit to. This build covers 100% free, universal General Perks , providing excellent tracking, chase assistance, and an essential endgame safety net.

  • Fearmonger / Mindbreaker (General Perk) – Automatically inflicts Blindness and Exhaustion when Survivors begin repairing a Generator.
  • Bitter Murmur (General Perk) – When a Generator is repaired, the Aura of any Survivor within 16 meters will be exposed.
  • Sloppy Butcher (General Perk) – Basic attacks cause the Mangled and Hemorrhage status ailments for 90 seconds, slowing down healing by 25% and regressing healing progress when left unfinished.
  • Hex: No One Escapes Death (General Perk) – After the final generator is completed, all Survivors suffer from the Exposed status ailment, and you gain a 4% Haste effect until the Hex Totem is cleansed.

Sloppy Butcher is a fine beginner option, but its effectiveness took a big hit when Dead by Daylight changed its modifier-stacking rules. You should try to replace it with a teachable regression perk as soon as possible. Resonance or Pop Goes the Weasel are solid options.

The Spirit: Best Build

Advanced The Spirit Build

With The Spirit's powerful mobility, perks such as Pop Goes The Weasel, Discordance, and Friends 'til The End synergize exceptionally well with her kit. When everything might look bleak, opt for No Way Out to reliably secure a victory.

  • Friends 'til The End (The Good Guy) – After hooking a Survivor that is not the Obsession, the latter is Exposed, and their aura is revealed for a brief period. When the Obsession is hooked, a random Survivor becomes the new Obsession, screaming and revealing their location.
  • Discordance (The Legion) – Generators being repaired by two or more Survivors within a large radius are highlighted in yellow. The first highlight triggers a notification, and the aura persists for another four seconds if the generator is no longer in range, or if only one Survivor is working on it.
  • Pop Goes The Weasel (The Clown) – After hooking a Survivor, kicking a generator within a set amount of time regresses the generator by a hefty amount.
  • No Way Out (The Trickster) – Hooking a Survivor accumulates a token, up to a maximum of four. Once the exit gates are powered, both of them will be blocked when a Survivor interacts with an exit gate. This will trigger a notification, and the duration of the block depends on the number of tokens, up to a maximum of one minute.

The Spirit Best Add-Ons

Gifted Bamboo Comb

While all The Spirit’s Common Add-Ons offer decent buffs to her base kit, perhaps the most effective is the Gifted Bamboo Comb. This power modifier speeds up the rate at which you charge Yamaoka’s Haunting by 15% , allowing you to enter phase faster in a chase.

White Hair Ribbon

Pairing well with the aforementioned Gifted Bamboo Comb, the White Hair Ribbon increases Yamaoka’s Haunting charge speed by 20% .

Origami Crane

A pretty simple Add-On; Origami Crane allows Rin to recharge her Haunting 20% faster, reducing her cooldown. This Add-On combines well with White Hair Ribbon, as they would ensure Rin gains access to her power as quickly as possible. Although not necessarily the most exciting build, this combo should work well for most The Spirit players.

Muddy Sports Day Cap

Speed is everything to The Spirit, as her Haunting makes her one of the fastest Killers in Dead by Daylight . The Muddy Sports Day Cap Add-On increases her movement speed during power by 10% .

Uchiwa

Simple, but effective. This Rare Spirit Add-On instantly recharges your power when hit with a pallet stun . This addon is great for Dead by Daylight beginners who often get stunned.

Rusty Flute

A stronger version of Rin’s Broken Watch, the Rusty Flute increases The Spirit’s power recharge rate by 40% .

Dried Cherry Blossom

The Spirit’s best Add-Ons match The Blight's in terms of transformative strength. The Dried Cherry Blossom reveals Survivors within 3 meters during power. This comes at the cost of no longer being able to see scratch marks from Survivor footsteps, so you should only run this if you’re already familiar with listening out for Survivors' breaths and injured noises.

Mother-Daughter Ring

Like the Dried Cherry Blossom, this Ultra Rare Add-On removes The Spirit’s ability to see scratch marks in power; however, she can move at a massive 25% extra speed as she phases. With this addon, you can catch up to Survivors incredibly quickly, provided you can hear them well enough.

Kintsugi Teacup

Once again, we have an Ultra Rare Add-On for advanced players, and Kintsugi Teacup is a pretty easy recommendation for anybody running an anti-loop strategy. Rin's Haunting instantly recharges after she breaks a wall or pallet .

How To Unlock The Spirit In Dead by Daylight

Dead by Daylight only has a couple of free killers, and this lineup has remained relatively stable throughout the game's run. Consequently, if you are interested in playing as Rin Yamaoka , you will need to buy her first. Now, fortunately, as she has been around for quite a long time (since 2018, to be exact), she qualifies for the cheapest bracket of DLC characters, costing 2,250 Iridescent Shards or 125 Auric Cells in the In-Game Store. Her original DLC, Shattered Bloodline, is available on the PC version, but it can be more expensive than buying the character individually since Rin often goes on sale.

If you just want The Spirit, the best option is to just buy her solo. Iridescent Shards can be earned by just playing the game and leveling up; yes, the process is quite slow, but you will eventually be able to pick up Rin. Auric Cells require investing real money, with the cheapest being $4.99 for 500.

The Spirit is also one of the Killers included in Dead by Daylight: Gold Edition , so you don't need to do anything else if you picked up that version.

Who’s That Character?

Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.

5.0 s
0 / 10

Results

0

High Score: 0

—

More Games
Who’s That Character? Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.
Easy (7.5s)Medium (5.0s)Hard (2.5s)Permadeath (2.5s)
5.0 s
0 / 10
Results
0
High Score: 0 —
More Games

The Spirit As Part Of DBD Packs

On PC, there is the Maddening Darkness Pack , which includes The Spirit along with The Hag, The Doctor, and The Clown. The package also has four Survivors (Ace Visconti, Feng Min, Kate Denson, and Adam Francis), along with a bunch of cosmetics (but none for Rin).

Please note that Maddening Darkness Pack is not available on consoles since a few of the characters are part of the base game, although The Spirit is not one of them.

Rin is also part of the Terror Expansion Pack , which comes with 11 Killers on PC and 9 Killers on console.

Is The Spirit A Good DBD Killer For Beginners?

The Spirit is a weird case that has a bit of a complex answer when it comes to deciding whether she is a newcomer-appropriate Killer, as the question can be answered in two ways.

Firstly, is Rin a difficult Dead by Daylight Killer to learn? Honestly, no. While not as beginner-friendly as someone like The Wraith, The Spirit is one of the simpler Killers in Dead by Daylight , especially since her typical strategy involves listening to Survivors and phasing, making her a great mind-games Killer. Put on some solid headphones, and you will already be at a strong advantage. Look out for scratch marks and use your phasing as freely as possible. Like with any Killer, you will need a few rounds to come to grips with Rin's moveset and strengths, but she is among the easiest characters to grasp.

So, in a vacuum, The Spirit is a good Killer for beginners. However, Dead by Daylight should not be played in a vacuum, and beginners should prioritize characters who can teach them the basics that hold for most Killers (or Survivors). The Spirit functions differently from most other Killers, so her strategies do not really translate since she relies so heavily on her phase-walking. Consequently, once you master The Spirit, your expertise will not go far beyond this one Killer, making it difficult to transition to another character.

Should Anybody Play As The Spirit In 2026?

Are There Similar But Better Killers?

OK, we have confirmed that The Spirit is a good Killer for beginners; however, her relative accessibility would amount to nothing if she isn't a viable option in the long term. If she were free, I would say just give her a try to see how you feel, but she needs to be purchased. Consequently, you will need to decide if she is worth the Iridescent Shards, especially since they are not very easy to come by. Currently, Rin is in the top 15 most-played Killers in the game, with a pick rate of just under 3%. While popular enough not to be completely forgotten, she is not currently part of the strongest meta. To be fair, The Spirit has never been part of the meta, just because she plays so differently than nearly every other Killer.

Yes, The Spirit is a good Killer in 2026 .

The Skill Ceiling vs. Reward Gap

The Spirit is an odd case. Although relatively easy to grasp, her moveset is so unique that it requires quite a bit of time to master, regardless of whether the player is a complete beginner or a veteran. An experienced and skilled Spirit can, arguably, end chases faster than most other Killers, even eclipsing somebody like Blight. As Dead by Daylight 's Survivor meta currently emphasizes exhaustion perk synergies, Rin is actually a great counter, as she almost bypasses these types of builds. Players will need to master directional audio tracking and post-phase lunges before they see consistent success, but that comes with practice.

Great Counter To The Visual Terror Radius

The Visual Terror Radius refers to a Survivor's heartbeat, which changes depending on a Killer's vicinity. This mechanic can be used by skilled players to identify a Killer's position or, at least, how far they might be. With a few Add-Ons like Rusty Flute or Trail of Torment, The Spirit can become completely undetectable, at least for a time. This makes her one of the more reliable anti-meta Killers, along with just being a fantastic option if you want to jump scare Survivors.

In 2026, Survivors know plenty of ways to ensure they maintain a safe distance during loops, which can be very hard for most M1 Killers to counter. However, this strategy doesn't work that well against The Spirit, as she doesn't rely on traditional environmental tools like pallets or windows (at least, not in the traditional way).

Similar But Better Killers?

"Better" is subjective, but the following Killers surpass The Spirit in certain ways.

  • The Ghoul – The Spirit relies on her mobility, and The Ghoul is currently the best speed Killer in the game. Kagune Leap rivals Spirit's phase, and The Ghoul doesn't go blind in the process.
  • The Hillbilly – If you are looking for a free alternative, Hillbilly is surprisingly viable in 2026. He has insta-down potential that The Spirit cannot replicate.
Dead by Daylight
Released
June 14, 2016

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Developer(s)
Behaviour Interactive
Platform(s)
PC, PS4, PS5, Xbox One, Xbox Series X, Xbox Series S, Stadia, Switch, Mobile
Genre(s)
Horror, Survival
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
Updated on June 18, 2026 by Mark Sammut: While Rin's kit remains the same, patch 9.6.0 introduced widespread changes that impact Spirit builds. We have added a section discussing these changes, along with tweaking the Beginner's Build and Add-Ons.

Dead by Daylight has over 40 Killers at the moment, and The Spirit has been around longer than the vast majority of them. While seemingly inspired by Japanese horror movies, Rin Yamaoka is an original character, making her more accessible than the licensed Killers. Debuting in 2018's Chapter 9, The Spirit is a staple of DBD 's gameplay scene, and she tends to show up in a decent number of matches. Considering she has not received a dedicated update since 2023, this shows that Rin is just an all-around consistent Killer who has carved out a niche for herself, one that does not need to be tweaked to make her a viable option.

The Spirit can vanish, throwing the Survivors off their tracking game, forcing them to rely on a different sense than usual. If you are a fairly new DBD player and are interested in giving Rin a try, you might need a crash course in her non-teachable perks to get you going with a beginner-friendly build. Once you spend time with the character and unlock further options, you will need to switch to more advanced The Spirit builds.

The Spirit: 2026 Changes

Dead by Daylight's Meta Went Through a Shake-Up Earlier In 2026

Behaviour Interactive hasn't directly updated Rin Yamaoka's baseline mechanics in years, and that seems unlikely to change anytime soon. However, in 2026, the developer implemented changes that impacted nearly every Killer and Survivor, albeit to different extents. The 9.6.0 patch was the biggest DBD update in a long time. Let's go over the main changes.

  • Stacking repeat modifiers now results in diminishing returns. Consequently, stacking multiple identical progression or healing slowdown mechanics now triggers harsh, progressive diminishing returns. The old-school passive Spirit loadouts that stacked slowdowns upon each other have lost a lot of their value. Nowadays, Spirit players should aim for an aggressive, high-tempo chase.
  • From now on, the Killer's identity is hidden at the start of a match, only being revealed to the Survivors once they enter a chase or lose a health state. For a high-mobility threat like Spirit, this is a massive early-game buffer; you can phase directly onto a generator at second zero and execute a clean ambush before survivors even realize they are playing against a phase-walking killer.

The Spirit: Best Beginner's Build

Straightforward Build For New Players

Though a strong Killer, The Spirit comes with a few impressive base kit perks, forcing her to rely on more tried and tested means of killing Survivors. Those just starting might not want to spend money or grind dozens of hours to build a great loadout for a Killer they might not even commit to. This build covers 100% free, universal General Perks , providing excellent tracking, chase assistance, and an essential endgame safety net.

  • Fearmonger / Mindbreaker (General Perk) – Automatically inflicts Blindness and Exhaustion when Survivors begin repairing a Generator.
  • Bitter Murmur (General Perk) – When a Generator is repaired, the Aura of any Survivor within 16 meters will be exposed.
  • Sloppy Butcher (General Perk) – Basic attacks cause the Mangled and Hemorrhage status ailments for 90 seconds, slowing down healing by 25% and regressing healing progress when left unfinished.
  • Hex: No One Escapes Death (General Perk) – After the final generator is completed, all Survivors suffer from the Exposed status ailment, and you gain a 4% Haste effect until the Hex Totem is cleansed.

Sloppy Butcher is a fine beginner option, but its effectiveness took a big hit when Dead by Daylight changed its modifier-stacking rules. You should try to replace it with a teachable regression perk as soon as possible. Resonance or Pop Goes the Weasel are solid options.

The Spirit: Best Build

Advanced The Spirit Build

With The Spirit's powerful mobility, perks such as Pop Goes The Weasel, Discordance, and Friends 'til The End synergize exceptionally well with her kit. When everything might look bleak, opt for No Way Out to reliably secure a victory.

  • Friends 'til The End (The Good Guy) – After hooking a Survivor that is not the Obsession, the latter is Exposed, and their aura is revealed for a brief period. When the Obsession is hooked, a random Survivor becomes the new Obsession, screaming and revealing their location.
  • Discordance (The Legion) – Generators being repaired by two or more Survivors within a large radius are highlighted in yellow. The first highlight triggers a notification, and the aura persists for another four seconds if the generator is no longer in range, or if only one Survivor is working on it.
  • Pop Goes The Weasel (The Clown) – After hooking a Survivor, kicking a generator within a set amount of time regresses the generator by a hefty amount.
  • No Way Out (The Trickster) – Hooking a Survivor accumulates a token, up to a maximum of four. Once the exit gates are powered, both of them will be blocked when a Survivor interacts with an exit gate. This will trigger a notification, and the duration of the block depends on the number of tokens, up to a maximum of one minute.

The Spirit Best Add-Ons

Gifted Bamboo Comb

While all The Spirit’s Common Add-Ons offer decent buffs to her base kit, perhaps the most effective is the Gifted Bamboo Comb. This power modifier speeds up the rate at which you charge Yamaoka’s Haunting by 15% , allowing you to enter phase faster in a chase.

White Hair Ribbon

Pairing well with the aforementioned Gifted Bamboo Comb, the White Hair Ribbon increases Yamaoka’s Haunting charge speed by 20% .

Origami Crane

A pretty simple Add-On; Origami Crane allows Rin to recharge her Haunting 20% faster, reducing her cooldown. This Add-On combines well with White Hair Ribbon, as they would ensure Rin gains access to her power as quickly as possible. Although not necessarily the most exciting build, this combo should work well for most The Spirit players.

Muddy Sports Day Cap

Speed is everything to The Spirit, as her Haunting makes her one of the fastest Killers in Dead by Daylight . The Muddy Sports Day Cap Add-On increases her movement speed during power by 10% .

Uchiwa

Simple, but effective. This Rare Spirit Add-On instantly recharges your power when hit with a pallet stun . This addon is great for Dead by Daylight beginners who often get stunned.

Rusty Flute

A stronger version of Rin’s Broken Watch, the Rusty Flute increases The Spirit’s power recharge rate by 40% .

Dried Cherry Blossom

The Spirit’s best Add-Ons match The Blight's in terms of transformative strength. The Dried Cherry Blossom reveals Survivors within 3 meters during power. This comes at the cost of no longer being able to see scratch marks from Survivor footsteps, so you should only run this if you’re already familiar with listening out for Survivors' breaths and injured noises.

Mother-Daughter Ring

Like the Dried Cherry Blossom, this Ultra Rare Add-On removes The Spirit’s ability to see scratch marks in power; however, she can move at a massive 25% extra speed as she phases. With this addon, you can catch up to Survivors incredibly quickly, provided you can hear them well enough.

Kintsugi Teacup

Once again, we have an Ultra Rare Add-On for advanced players, and Kintsugi Teacup is a pretty easy recommendation for anybody running an anti-loop strategy. Rin's Haunting instantly recharges after she breaks a wall or pallet .

How To Unlock The Spirit In Dead by Daylight

Dead by Daylight only has a couple of free killers, and this lineup has remained relatively stable throughout the game's run. Consequently, if you are interested in playing as Rin Yamaoka , you will need to buy her first. Now, fortunately, as she has been around for quite a long time (since 2018, to be exact), she qualifies for the cheapest bracket of DLC characters, costing 2,250 Iridescent Shards or 125 Auric Cells in the In-Game Store. Her original DLC, Shattered Bloodline, is available on the PC version, but it can be more expensive than buying the character individually since Rin often goes on sale.

If you just want The Spirit, the best option is to just buy her solo. Iridescent Shards can be earned by just playing the game and leveling up; yes, the process is quite slow, but you will eventually be able to pick up Rin. Auric Cells require investing real money, with the cheapest being $4.99 for 500.

The Spirit is also one of the Killers included in Dead by Daylight: Gold Edition , so you don't need to do anything else if you picked up that version.

The Spirit As Part Of DBD Packs

On PC, there is the Maddening Darkness Pack , which includes The Spirit along with The Hag, The Doctor, and The Clown. The package also has four Survivors (Ace Visconti, Feng Min, Kate Denson, and Adam Francis), along with a bunch of cosmetics (but none for Rin).

Please note that Maddening Darkness Pack is not available on consoles since a few of the characters are part of the base game, although The Spirit is not one of them.

Rin is also part of the Terror Expansion Pack , which comes with 11 Killers on PC and 9 Killers on console.

Is The Spirit A Good DBD Killer For Beginners?

The Spirit is a weird case that has a bit of a complex answer when it comes to deciding whether she is a newcomer-appropriate Killer, as the question can be answered in two ways.

Firstly, is Rin a difficult Dead by Daylight Killer to learn? Honestly, no. While not as beginner-friendly as someone like The Wraith, The Spirit is one of the simpler Killers in Dead by Daylight , especially since her typical strategy involves listening to Survivors and phasing, making her a great mind-games Killer. Put on some solid headphones, and you will already be at a strong advantage. Look out for scratch marks and use your phasing as freely as possible. Like with any Killer, you will need a few rounds to come to grips with Rin's moveset and strengths, but she is among the easiest characters to grasp.

So, in a vacuum, The Spirit is a good Killer for beginners. However, Dead by Daylight should not be played in a vacuum, and beginners should prioritize characters who can teach them the basics that hold for most Killers (or Survivors). The Spirit functions differently from most other Killers, so her strategies do not really translate since she relies so heavily on her phase-walking. Consequently, once you master The Spirit, your expertise will not go far beyond this one Killer, making it difficult to transition to another character.

Should Anybody Play As The Spirit In 2026?

Are There Similar But Better Killers?

OK, we have confirmed that The Spirit is a good Killer for beginners; however, her relative accessibility would amount to nothing if she isn't a viable option in the long term. If she were free, I would say just give her a try to see how you feel, but she needs to be purchased. Consequently, you will need to decide if she is worth the Iridescent Shards, especially since they are not very easy to come by. Currently, Rin is in the top 15 most-played Killers in the game, with a pick rate of just under 3%. While popular enough not to be completely forgotten, she is not currently part of the strongest meta. To be fair, The Spirit has never been part of the meta, just because she plays so differently than nearly every other Killer.

Yes, The Spirit is a good Killer in 2026 .

The Skill Ceiling vs. Reward Gap

The Spirit is an odd case. Although relatively easy to grasp, her moveset is so unique that it requires quite a bit of time to master, regardless of whether the player is a complete beginner or a veteran. An experienced and skilled Spirit can, arguably, end chases faster than most other Killers, even eclipsing somebody like Blight. As Dead by Daylight 's Survivor meta currently emphasizes exhaustion perk synergies, Rin is actually a great counter, as she almost bypasses these types of builds. Players will need to master directional audio tracking and post-phase lunges before they see consistent success, but that comes with practice.

Great Counter To The Visual Terror Radius

The Visual Terror Radius refers to a Survivor's heartbeat, which changes depending on a Killer's vicinity. This mechanic can be used by skilled players to identify a Killer's position or, at least, how far they might be. With a few Add-Ons like Rusty Flute or Trail of Torment, The Spirit can become completely undetectable, at least for a time. This makes her one of the more reliable anti-meta Killers, along with just being a fantastic option if you want to jump scare Survivors.

In 2026, Survivors know plenty of ways to ensure they maintain a safe distance during loops, which can be very hard for most M1 Killers to counter. However, this strategy doesn't work that well against The Spirit, as she doesn't rely on traditional environmental tools like pallets or windows (at least, not in the traditional way).

Similar But Better Killers?

"Better" is subjective, but the following Killers surpass The Spirit in certain ways.

  • The Ghoul – The Spirit relies on her mobility, and The Ghoul is currently the best speed Killer in the game. Kagune Leap rivals Spirit's phase, and The Ghoul doesn't go blind in the process.
  • The Hillbilly – If you are looking for a free alternative, Hillbilly is surprisingly viable in 2026. He has insta-down potential that The Spirit cannot replicate.
